docs.title: bubseek
docs.text:
A Bub distribution for data-driven insight workflows, powered by OceanBase seekdb.

bubseek is an attempt to explore a different approach to enterprise data needs: instead of scheduling BI tickets, tell the agent what you want and get insights back.

docs.title: Start here
docs.text: Section What it answers Getting started Install, configure model/database, verify setup Configuration Channel credentials, skills, runtime options Architecture What bubseek does, and what it leaves to Bub Development Test, build, and work on this repo

docs.title: Quick links
docs.text:
• Repository
  • Bub
  • OceanBase/seekdb

docs.title: What bubseek does
docs.text:
bubseek is an attempt to explore a different approach to enterprise data needs: instead of scheduling BI tickets, tell the agent what you want and get insights back.

  • Packages Bub with pre-configured dependencies
  • Provides OceanBase/seekdb storage support via bub-tapestore-sqlalchemy
  • Ships builtin channels: Feishu, DingTalk, WeChat, Discord, Telegram, Marimo
  • Ships builtin skills: github-repo-cards, web-search, schedule
  • Provides built-in observability: agent's own footprint becomes queryable data

docs.title: What bubseek does not do
docs.text:
• It does not fork Bub
  • It does not define custom formats
  • It does not replace Bub's CLI

docs.title: Responsibility split
docs.text: Component Responsibility Bub Runtime, CLI, extension model, tape design bubseek Packaging, defaults, plugin wiring, skills Python packaging Dependencies, installation seekdb Storage for tapes, sessions, tasks

docs.title: Data flow
docs.text:
All data (tapes, sessions, tasks) flows through bub-tapestore-sqlalchemy into a single seekdb database. This enables:

  1. External observability — Agent serves team requests, produces insights
  2. Internal observability — Agent's own footprint (tapes) becomes data for analysis

The same agent that serves the team can also analyze its own history to understand: what questions are most frequent? What tasks fail often? What does the team care about?

docs.title: Philosophy
docs.text: bubseek explores a different approach: instead of scheduling BI tickets, tell the agent what you want and get insights back. All agent interactions are stored as tapes in seekdb, creating a feedback loop where the agent can analyze its own footprint.

docs.title: Model (required)
docs.text: See Getting started for model configuration.

docs.title: Database (required for tape storage)
docs.text: See Getting started for database configuration.

docs.title: Feishu
docs.text:
1. Visit https://open.feishu.cn/app → Create or select app
  2. Go to "Credentials & Basic Info" → Get App ID and App Secret
  3. Configure event subscriptions and permissions as needed
export BUB_FEISHU_APP_ID=cli_xxx
export BUB_FEISHU_APP_SECRET=xxx
uv run bub gateway --enable-channel feishu

docs.title: DingTalk
docs.text:
1. Visit https://open-dev.dingtalk.com/ → Create or select app
  2. Go to app details → Get Client ID and Client Secret
export BUB_DINGTALK_CLIENT_ID=xxx
export BUB_DINGTALK_CLIENT_SECRET=xxx
uv run bub gateway --enable-channel dingtalk

docs.title: Discord
docs.text:
1. Visit https://discord.com/developers/applications → New Application
  2. Go to "Bot" → Add Bot
  3. Click "Reset Token" to get bot token
  4. Enable "Message Content Intent" in Bot settings
export BUB_DISCORD_TOKEN=your-bot-token
uv run bub gateway --enable-channel discord

docs.title: Telegram
docs.text:
1. Open Telegram, search @BotFather
  2. Send /newbot and follow instructions
  3. BotFather returns the token
export BUB_TELEGRAM_TOKEN=your-bot-token
uv run bub gateway --enable-channel telegram

docs.title: WeChat
docs.text:
Interactive login required:

uv run bub login wechat    # Scan QR code with WeChat app
uv run bub gateway --enable-channel wechat

docs.title: Marimo (web dashboard)
docs.text:
export BUB_MARIMO_HOST=127.0.0.1  # optional
export BUB_MARIMO_PORT=2718       # optional
uv run bub gateway --enable-channel marimo

Visit http://127.0.0.1:2718 after enabling.

docs.title: Other settings
docs.text: Variable Description BUB_HOME Data directory (default: ~/.bub) BUB_MAX_STEPS Max steps per conversation BUB_MAX_TOKENS Max tokens per response BUB_SEARCH_OLLAMA_API_KEY For web search tool BUB_WORKSPACE_PATH Workspace directory

docs.title: Add contrib packages
docs.text:
Treat contrib as ordinary Python packages:

[project]
dependencies = [
    "bub-codex @ git+https://github.com/bubbuild/bub-contrib.git@main#subdirectory=packages/bub-codex",
]

Then run uv sync.

docs.title: Builtin skills
docs.text:
Skills are packaged in the wheel. No extra sync step needed.

  • github-repo-cards — generate repo summary cards
  • web-search — search the web
  • schedule — cron-style task scheduling
  • friendly-python, piglet — from PsiACE/skills
  • plugin-creator — from bub-contrib

docs.title: Marimo dashboard
docs.text:
Run uv run bub gateway --enable-channel marimo, then visit http://127.0.0.1:2718

Notebooks are generated to insights/ at runtime. The dashboard shows agent's own footprint — tapes, sessions, task history — enabling the agent to analyze itself.

docs.title: Development
docs.text: This page covers local development for the bubseek repository.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
From the repository root:

uv sync
make install

make install creates the virtual environment, installs dependencies, and installs pre-commit hooks.

docs.title: Common commands
docs.text: Command Description make install Install dependencies and pre-commit hooks. make check Run lock verification, linting, formatting checks, and type checks. make test Run pytest. make build Build the wheel and source distribution. make docs Serve documentation locally. make docs-test Build documentation and fail on warnings.

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
make test

Or directly:

uv run pytest tests

docs.title: Building
docs.text:
make build

The wheel includes builtin skills from skills/. The source files for those builtin skills live in src/skills/.

docs.title: Docs
docs.text:
make docs
make docs-test

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: See CONTRIBUTING.md.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text: bubseek is an attempt to explore a different approach to enterprise data needs: instead of scheduling BI tickets, tell the agent what you want and get insights back.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.12+ — Check with python3 --version
  • uv — Install with: bash curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/ob-labs/bubseek.git
cd bubseek
uv sync

Verify:

uv run bub --help

docs.title: Configure model (required)
docs.text: bubseek needs an LLM API. Choose a provider and get your API key:

docs.title: OpenRouter (recommended)
docs.text:
Unified access to many models with a single API key.

  1. Visit https://openrouter.ai/ → Sign in
  2. Go to https://openrouter.ai/keys → Create Key
export BUB_MODEL=openrouter:qwen/qwen3-coder-next
export BUB_API_KEY=sk-or-v1-your-key-here
export BUB_API_BASE=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1

docs.title: OpenAI
docs.text:
1. Visit https://platform.openai.com/ → Sign up
  2. Go to https://platform.openai.com/api-keys → Create new secret key
export BUB_MODEL=openai:gpt-4o
export BUB_API_KEY=sk-your-key-here
export BUB_API_BASE=https://api.openai.com/v1

docs.title: Custom OpenAI-compatible API
docs.text:
For self-hosted models or other providers:

export BUB_MODEL=openai:your-model-name
export BUB_API_KEY=your-key-here
export BUB_API_BASE=https://your-api-endpoint/v1

docs.title: OceanBase/seekdb
docs.text:
Deploy with Docker:

# Quick start (for testing)
docker run -d -p 2881:2881 oceanbase/seekdb

# With data persistence
mkdir -p seekdb
docker run -d -p 2881:2881 -v $PWD/seekdb:/var/lib/oceanbase --name seekdb oceanbase/seekdb

Connect and create database:

mysql -uroot -h127.0.0.1 -P2881 -p

CREATE DATABASE bubseek;

Configure:

export BUB_TAPESTORE_SQLALCHEMY_URL=mysql+oceanbase://root@127.0.0.1:2881/bubseek

For detailed deployment options, see OceanBase Docker deployment guide.

docs.title: Quickstart verification
docs.text:
Run this to verify your setup:

uv run bub chat

You should see the agent start. Try asking: "Hello, what can you do?"

If errors occur, check: 1. API key is correct and has credits 2. Database connection is valid 3. Network connectivity

docs.title: Enable channels (optional)
docs.text: See Configuration for channel setup details.
